docs/Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Environment (PVE)/index.md and child pages PvP rules docs/Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Player (PVP)/index.md Arena rules docs/Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Player (PVP)/Arena.md Events docs/Gameplay/Events/index.md and child pages Sectors, collision, and following docs/World/Maps/Sectors.md Spawnables, exploration, security rating docs/World/Maps/ Factions docs/World/Factions/index.md and child pages Structures docs/Entities/Structures/index.md and child pages Artifacts docs/Entities/Structures/Artifacts.md Corporations and alliances docs/Entities/Corporations/index.md and child pages Currencies docs/Entities/Currencies/index.md and child pages New player flows and settings docs/Getting-Started/ Quick-start guides docs/Getting-Started/Quick-Start-Guides/ Strategy and community tactics docs/Strategy/

Legacy/duplicate entity pages should be treated carefully. If a mechanic exists in both docs/Ships/ and docs/Entities/Ships/, or both docs/Items/ and docs/Entities/Items/, prefer the newer top-level docs/Ships/ and docs/Items/ canonical pages unless we deliberately decide to reorganize that section. Do not expand both copies with the same explanation.

Short summaries are fine, but they should point to the canonical page for details.

docs.title: Repetition rule
docs.text:
Before adding a paragraph, ask:

  • Does a dedicated page already explain this?
  • Would changing this information later require editing more than one page?
  • Is this page trying to be a guide, a reference, or an index?

If the answer is yes, shorten the text and link out.

Good pattern:

Equip Points limit how much gear a ship can mount. See [Equip Points](../Ships/Equip-Points.md).

Avoid:

Equip Points are the capacity your ship has for weapons, armor, shields...

unless the page itself is the Equip Points page.

When a page needs context, use one or two sentences and link to the canonical page. If the content would require a table, formula, full rule list, or exception list, it belongs on the canonical page.

Good pattern:

Ship construction can require corp membership time, relics, Skulls, resources, and a valid Shipyard. See [Ship Crafting](../Ships/Ship-Crafting.md).

Avoid copying the full ship construction gate stack into corporation, relic, skull, and shipyard pages.

docs.title: Rules vs strategy
docs.text:
Reference/gameplay pages explain what the game does. Strategy pages explain what players recommend doing.

Put content in a rule page when it answers:

  • What is this system?
  • What are the requirements?
  • What does the client validate?
  • What numbers or formulas are known?
  • What is still unknown?

Put content in docs/Strategy/ when it answers:

  • What should I build?
  • What is best or efficient?
  • How do players usually counter this?
  • What is the current meta?
  • What should a new player prioritize?

Rule pages may have a See also link to strategy. Strategy pages may summarize a mechanic briefly, then link back to the canonical rule page.

docs.title: Accuracy rule
docs.text:
Do not present guesses as facts.

Use player-facing rarity names in public wiki text and tables: Common, Uncommon, Rare, Ultra-Rare, Elite, Legendary, and Ultimate.

Internal enum labels may appear in hidden contributor extraction notes only when quoting or preserving raw source context. Do not use numbered rarity shorthand in public pages.

Use one of these labels when data is not fully verified:

  • Needs data for missing values.
  • Estimated for community-tested but not official values.
  • Observed for values seen in game but not confirmed as universal.
  • Source needed for claims copied from a rough pass without a clear source.

Remove placeholder numbers rather than publishing them as if they were real.

Use these source tags sparingly when they help:

  • Client-confirmed for rules directly supported by the current client extraction.
  • Observed for live behavior seen in game.
  • Community guidance for advice or meta claims.
  • Needs confirmation for unclear decompiled branches, server-side uncertainty, or source conflicts.

Avoid filling public pages with source tags on every sentence. Use them where the reader could otherwise mistake uncertainty for a settled rule.

docs.title: Training-data rule
docs.text:
When converting training data into wiki content:

  • Keep durable mechanics.
  • Avoid copying personal opinions into reference pages.
  • Convert advice into neutral guidance.
  • Preserve source nuance when data is uncertain.
  • Prefer a dedicated guide page for strategy-heavy Discord notes.

Discord training data is useful, but it is rough. Treat it as community notes until confirmed.

docs.title: Authored guide prose
docs.text:
Some Discord training-data sections were written as polished guide prose by known contributors. In particular, cattabliss is a professional writer and has written several guide sections that should be respected as authored work.

When a cattabliss section fits a wiki page:

  • Preserve the wording as-is where practical.
  • Keep the structure and explanatory flow when adapting to Markdown.
  • Only edit for accuracy, links, formatting, or to avoid duplicating a canonical page.
  • If the text is strategy or opinion-heavy, place it in a guide page rather than diluting it into reference prose.

docs.title: Link rule
docs.text:
Use Markdown links to .md source files. For paths with parentheses, wrap the destination in angle brackets:

[PvP](<../Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Player (PVP)/index.md>)

See Markdown Links for the full linking guide.

docs.title: Integration checklist
docs.text:
Use this checklist when moving contributor notes into public pages:

  1. Pick the canonical page from the map above.
  2. Check whether the same rule already exists elsewhere.
  3. Move or rewrite the full rule only on the canonical page.
  4. Replace duplicate explanations with short summaries and links.
  5. Keep strategy out of rule pages.
  6. Mark unresolved formulas or suspicious decompiled branches as Needs confirmation or Needs data.
  7. Preserve cattabliss guide prose where it fits, especially in guide or strategy pages.
  8. Add See also links instead of repeating connected mechanics.
  9. Update navigation only when a page should be public and easy to browse.
  10. Leave contributor mining notes hidden unless they are being intentionally surfaced.

docs.title: Page shape
docs.text:
Most pages should follow this pattern:

  1. One-sentence definition.
  2. Core rules or table.
  3. Practical notes only if they are stable.
  4. Contributor note if data is missing or uncertain.
  5. See also links to canonical related pages.

Index pages should summarize and link. They should not become duplicate reference pages.

docs.title: Draft Notes
docs.text: This file is hidden, so it won't show up in the deployed nav bar, but it is still searchable. Check Contributing/.pages for an example of how to hide pages from the nav.

docs.title: FAQ
docs.text: Commonly asked questions about folder structure should go here.

docs.title: Gaps to Fill -- Information to Collect
docs.text: Use this as a checklist. For each gap, collect the information listed; once you have it, we can update the wiki.

docs.title: 1. Security Rating (World/Maps/Security Rating.md)
docs.text:
What we need: Fill the remaining non-client fields for each SR (1-9). The current client now confirms broad NPC level bands; we still need observed sector counts and practical readiness guidance.

For each Security Rating (SR1 through SR9), please collect:

Field Example (current placeholder) Your data NPC ship level range Client band exists; verify if live behavior differs Max NPC ships per sector e.g. SR1: 3 Recommended player level range e.g. 1-10 Recommended ship hull tier e.g. T1-T2 Any special notes e.g. "Starter-friendly"

Where to get it: In-game observation, dev/patch notes, or community notes. If exact numbers aren't available, "best guess" ranges are fine -- we'll mark them as indicative.

docs.title: 2. Equip Points (Ships/Equip-Points.md)
docs.text:
What we need: Add verified Equip Point tables or examples when available.

Client extraction update: The current client confirms that a gear set's required EP is max(0, sum(item EP cost) - sum(item EP bonus)). It also gives base ship EP by ship class and faction, plus item EP cost formulas for several item families. We still need exact item rank/base Ep tables and defense-unit EP max tables.

Please collect (if available):

Item Notes EP by ship class How many Equip Points each ship class (or size) has (e.g. Shuttle 10, Frigate 15, ...). EP by ship tier/level Does EP change with ship level or tier? Defense unit EP How EP works for Planets, Garrisons, Outposts, Shipyards (base and per level if applicable). Item rank/base Ep tables The formulas are known, but exact rank/base Ep values for weapons, armor, storage, and shields are still needed.

Where to get remaining data: In-game item inspect, defense unit inspect, in-game wiki, HELP, or deeper client extraction. Ship base EP is now source-backed in Client-Extraction-Notes.md.

docs.title: 3. Technology (Gameplay/Technology.md)
docs.text:
What we need: Enough to replace "We'll add a categorized overview here" with real content.

Client extraction update: The current client contains the technology enum, display names, descriptions, daily-tech descriptions, and helper methods for stackability/research/artifact/boost/defense eligibility. A raw source-backed staging table now exists in Client-Extracted-Technology.md.

Please collect (any that you can):

Item Notes Categories How techs are grouped in-game (e.g. Hull, Weapons, Movement, Crafting). Public grouping Decide which client-extracted techs should become public reference pages versus advanced/strategy notes. Ranked vs non-ranked Helper methods exist in the client, but still need to be mined into a public-ready table. Sources Which techs come from Artifacts vs corp research vs items vs ships (brief list or examples).

Where to get remaining data: Client helper-method extraction for flags, plus in-game HELP/in-game wiki for public grouping and source wording.

docs.title: 4. Skills (Gameplay/Skills.md)
docs.text:
What we need: Enough to replace "Full skill list and recommendations will be added here" with real content.

Client extraction update: The current client confirms the skill-upgrade stat list and basic/advanced mod-per-point values. We still need public wording and any max-point/point-source rules before replacing the page.

Please collect (any that you can):

Item Notes Skill list All (or main) captain/ship/corp skills and what they do (e.g. +XP%, Evasion, Hull). Max points per stat e.g. "Up to 10 points per stat, reduced benefit above 5." New-player recommendations 3-5 "get these first" skills (e.g. +XP%, Evasion) and why. Where points come from Leveling, Artifacts, etc. (we already mention Artifacts; confirm or add).

Where to get it: In-game skill tree, in-game wiki, or community guides.

docs.title: 5. Weapons (Items/Weapons.md) -- optional
docs.text:
What we need: Only if you want to go beyond the current overview.

Client extraction update: Weapon class display names, alternate variant names, and faction mappings are now staged in Client-Extracted-Items.md. We still need the gameplay meaning of the alternate weapon-name argument and exact stat/EP values.

Stat extraction update: Runtime item-family stat coverage is staged in Client-Extracted-Item-Stat-Coverage.md. We now know which item families have client logic for each stat, but not yet the normalized per-class formulas.

Formula extraction update: Raw shield, engine, and battery formula bodies are staged in Client-Extracted-Item-Stat-Formulas-Narrow.md. These still need helper-method translation and public wording.

Please collect (optional):

Item Notes Damage types e.g. Kinetic, Energy, etc., if the game shows them. Weapon tiers/ranks How weapon rank affects damage or EP cost. Formula normalization Turn the client stat override methods into clean per-family/per-class tables.

Where to get it: In-game weapon stats, item inspect, or wiki.

docs.title: 6. Starmap (optional new page)
docs.text:
What we need: Only if you want a dedicated Starmap page (we already have World index + Maps).

Please collect (optional):

Item Notes System list Names of solar systems (or "50+ systems" and a few examples). How to open Starmap e.g. "Hamburger menu -> Starmap". What the map shows Faction colors, security, jump gates, etc. (we have some of this in Maps; any extra).

Where to get it: In-game Starmap UI and in-game wiki.

docs.title: 7. Player Guides (Getting Started/Player-Guides.md) -- optional
docs.text:
What we need: Only if you want to remove the "Guides in progress" box and lock the list.

Item Notes Final list Which community guides to keep (with titles + URLs). Short description One line per guide (e.g. "New player basics", "Gear and builds").

docs.title: Quick reference: where each goes
docs.text: Gap Wiki page to update Security Rating data docs/World/Maps/Security Rating.md Equip Points details docs/Ships/Equip-Points.md Technology list/categories docs/Gameplay/Technology.md Skills list/recommendations docs/Gameplay/Skills.md Weapons damage/tiers (optional) docs/Items/Weapons.md Starmap (optional) New: docs/World/Starmap.md or expand docs/World/index.md Player guides list (optional) docs/Getting-Started/Player-Guides.md

docs.title: Priority order (suggested)
docs.text:
1. Security Rating -- One table per SR; high impact for players.
  2. Equip Points -- A few verified numbers or examples so we can expand the page beyond the basic source-backed overview.
  3. Technology -- Categories + list (or export) so we can add an overview.
  4. Skills -- List + new-player tips so we can add content.
  5. Weapons / Starmap / Player guides -- Optional; collect if you have time.

When you have the data, you can paste it here (or in a comment) and we can turn it into wiki text and update the pages.

docs.title: Gootchat Source Review
docs.text:
Review of Gathered Training Data/gootchat.txt, a raw export of Goot/Gooter/Gaytor outgoing in-game messages across channels.

This is a high-value community teaching source, but it is still chat. Treat it as practical guidance and lead-player observations unless confirmed by the in-game wiki, current testing, or direct developer data.

docs.title: What this source improves
docs.text: Topic What gootchat helps define Suggested wiki destination Carrier / Leadership Level 35 carrier milestone, carrier bonus requiring corporation membership, fleet move speed inheriting carrier movement, carrier gearing priority: speed first, survivability second. Ships/Carrier-Leadership.md. Item slot priority First gun/drone/battery slot gives full item stats; later slots are "aux" and only give 10%. Technologies and main item actions still apply in full. Items/Equipment-Slots.md. Interdictors Enemy interdictors nerf target speed and move speed; target speed is especially important in PvP; world-sector visual cues appear to distinguish enemy vs friendly interdictor influence. Ships/Interdictor-Carrier.md, World/Maps/Sectors.md. Outpost missions Mission tab + question mark can identify the source map/outpost. Outpost tasks can include kills and resource deposit steps. Resource deposits must go to the issuing outpost. Gameplay/Missions.md, possible Gameplay/Activities/Outpost-Missions.md. Structure gear score Structures need minimum gear score to deploy; PvP structure skull payout is based on Gear Score. Low-GS-but-effective structures are used to reduce enemy payout. Entities/Structures/index.md. Freighter Freighter technology improves harvesting; gootchat describes doubled harvest yield, while the in-game wiki describes doubled harvesting chances. Ships/Ship-Mods.md, Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Environment (PVE)/Open World Farming.md. PvP sorting and drivers Drivers can be targeted by sort order; "blend in" gearing matters; common concerns include small hull, worst gear, largest size, fastest, first to arrive, most in danger. PvP guide pages, not core reference pages. Settings Local sector ship count and top-down camera view are repeatedly explained to players. New Getting-Started/Settings.md. Follow behavior Follows drop as soon as the followed ship is 3 sectors away and cannot resume until close enough. World/Maps/Sectors.md. Wyrd shield builds Wyrd ships have stable shield-regeneration and shield-capacitor behavior, with practical farming implications. World/Factions/Wyrd.md; deeper guide later.

docs.title: Likely new pages from this source
docs.text: Page Why it may be useful Getting-Started/Settings.md Repeated practical advice: local sector counts, top-down view, sort options, camera behavior. Ships/Carrier-Leadership.md Carrier behavior is important enough for new players that it may deserve a focused page instead of being buried in Ships index. Items/Equipment-Slots.md First-slot vs aux-slot rules are core, easy to miss, and affect every build. Gameplay/Activities/Outpost-Missions.md Outpost missions are distinct enough from general missions to deserve a guide. Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Player (PVP)/Fleet-vs-Fleet-Guide.md Good place for driver, sort-order, collision, and interdictor strategy without polluting reference pages.

docs.title: Confirmed by Goot
docs.text: Claim Confirmation Carrier bonus only works when the player is in a corporation. Confirmed. Carrier-led fleets inherit carrier movement pacing. Confirmed. Hard minimum move times are 3.0 s small, 3.2 s medium, 3.3 s large, and 3.5 s huge/massive. Confirmed. Ships can overstack move speed past the hard minimum to counter enemy Interdictor penalties. Confirmed. Aux slots give 10% of all stats on that item. Confirmed. Technologies from aux-slot items apply in full. Confirmed. Weapon/drone main actions are not reduced by aux-slot status. Confirmed. Follow drops as soon as the followed ship is 3 sectors away. Confirmed. Interdictor UI cues: enemy is bright red/pulsing, friendly is light gray pulsing ring. Confirmed. Outpost missions should have a dedicated page. Confirmed. Wyrd shield mechanics are stable enough to document. Confirmed.

docs.title: Claims still needing exact source or formula
docs.text: Claim Why it needs confirmation Freighter technology makes each harvest from that ship yield 2x normal. Likely useful, but confirm whether it is exactly 2x and whether it applies to all harvested resources. Largest-size sort is EP-based rather than ship class alone. Important PvP sorting behavior; needs exact definition.

docs.title: Content guidance
docs.text:
Use gootchat mostly for:

  • practical player-facing guides
  • workflow steps players repeatedly ask for
  • examples of confusing UI
  • strategy notes labeled as community guidance

Avoid using gootchat alone for:

  • exact formulas
  • market prices
  • current meta builds
  • balance claims
  • "best" recommendations without context

docs.title: Immediate wiki opportunities
docs.text:
1. Add a Settings page for new players.
  2. Expand Carrier Leadership with examples if needed.
  3. Expand Equipment Slots with screenshots or examples if needed.
  4. Expand Outpost Missions with rewards and chain rules once exact values are verified.
  5. Expand Interdictor Carrier with rank math when confirmed.

docs.title: Wiki Fill-Out Plan
docs.text:
This old fill-out plan has been retired. It described the original wiki buildout and now contains too much completed or outdated routing information to use as guidance.

Use these current pages instead:

  • Content Standards -- canonical-page rules, source handling, and strategy separation.
  • Wiki Rough-Pass Audit -- current risks, remaining data gaps, and cleanup notes.
  • Gootchat Source Review -- notes from gootchat.txt.

When adding new content, choose the canonical section first:

  • Ships/ for ships, movement, ship mods, and ship crafting.
  • Items/ for item types, stats, upgrades, Specials, and Tech Computers.
  • Gameplay/ for stable activity rules and ability mechanics.
  • Strategy/ for build advice, tactics, and community guidance.
  • World/ for maps, sectors, security rating, and factions.

docs.title: Wiki Review
docs.text:
This review has been retired. It was a snapshot from an earlier build phase and should not be used as a live missing-content list.

Current gap tracking lives in:

  • Wiki Rough-Pass Audit
  • Gaps To Fill Checklist
  • Content Standards

docs.title: Wiki Rough-Pass Audit
docs.text: This page records the first broad cleanup pass across the wiki and training data. It is not a final accuracy review. Treat it as a working list of risks and next actions.

docs.title: Cleanup completed
docs.text:
• Normalized Markdown and YAML text to plain ASCII punctuation to avoid display issues in terminals and editors.
  • Wrapped internal Markdown links whose paths contain parentheses, such as Player vs Player (PVP), in angle brackets.
  • Replaced the unsupported Security Rating placeholder table with a source-backed page and a data-needed table.
  • Expanded Equip Points, Technology, and Skills enough to remove "coming soon" language while keeping detailed mechanics on their canonical pages.
  • Added player-written quick-start guides for cattabliss systems material and LuckyMFer's Arena notes.
  • Added canonical pages for recommended settings, common chat commands, and Fleet PvP operations.
  • Hid old empty duplicate Entities/Items, Entities/Ships, and unused corporation stub pages from navigation while leaving files in place for a later deletion/redirect decision.
  • Added Pass 2 new-player workflow pages: Getting-Started/Common-Workflows.md, expanded How-To-Play.md, and tightened FAQ answers to link to canonical pages.
  • Added Pass 3 PvE/economy pages: expanded Gameplay/Missions.md, added Open World Farming.md and NPC Busts.md, filled PvE navigation, and removed fixed market-price guidance from currency pages.
  • Added Pass 4 PvP/Alliance War pages: filled Structure Busts.md as a rules page, added PvP navigation, expanded Skulls.md with observed open-world reward rules, and moved PvP/structure strategy into the root Strategy/ section.
  • Added Pass 5 advanced pages: Items/Specials.md, Items/Tech-Computers.md, Strategy/Build-Philosophy.md, Strategy/Computer-Deconstruction.md, and Strategy/Faction-Ship-Notes.md. Kept exact formulas as Needs data where current sources are guide/chat notes rather than verified mechanics.
  • Added cleanup pass after the five planned passes: removed root catch-all navigation, curated World/Maps navigation, hid legacy folders, emptied hidden placeholder files so they are ready for future data, and added contributor changelog notes.

docs.title: Major structural findings
docs.text: Finding Risk Suggested action Legacy pages exist under docs/Entities/Ships/, docs/Entities/Items/, and docs/New Player Guide/. Contributors may edit the wrong location or create duplicate pages. Fixed for now: these are hidden from navigation and now point to canonical pages instead of being empty. Decide later whether to delete them or keep them as redirect-style search landing pages. Placeholder pages exist in miscellaneous entities, PvP activity subpages, exploration, and spawnables. Empty pages may appear in search or future navigation without useful content. Fixed for now: hidden placeholder files are intentionally empty until sourced. Old planning docs still exist for old links. Contributors may follow old tasks that are already complete. Fixed for now: Wiki-Fill-Out-Plan.md, Wiki-Status.md, and Wiki-Review-What-We-Are-Missing.md are short retired notes and are no longer in contributor navigation. Several pages summarize mechanics that have dedicated pages. Future changes can drift when the same rule is edited in one place but not another. Apply the canonical page rule in Content Standards.

docs.title: Information likely unsupported or needing verification
docs.text: Page Claim or area Why it is risky Action World/Maps/Security Rating.md Exact NPC counts and recommended hull tiers. Current client now confirms broad NPC level bands, but does not provide observed ship counts or player-ready recommendations. Added client level bands; keep counts and recommendations as "Needs data." Entities/Currencies/Skulls.md Typical market value of ~700-800 Credits per Skull. Market values are time-sensitive and not durable wiki data. Fixed: replaced with general market-dependent language. Entities/Currencies/Star Shards.md Star Shard exchange details and cosmetic uses. The in-game wiki export in this repo does not appear to cover Star Shards in detail. Verify in game before treating as canonical. Ships/Ship-Mods.md / Freighter Exact Freighter harvesting behavior. In-game wiki says "doubles harvesting chances"; gootchat says "2x normal amount". Left as harvesting improvement with Needs data note in Open World Farming until confirmed. Gameplay/Oblivion-Server.md Specific death, durability, and loot multiplier values. Checked against the in-game wiki export. Fixed: page now reflects the exported Oblivion rules without source-facing wording. Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Player (PVP)/Arena.md Arena schedule, rewards, modes, and gear-score thresholds. In-game wiki gives broad Arena rules; Discord notes add more detail and may be current but should be treated as community data. Fixed for now: 1,000 Gear Score is documented as the queue minimum; full-reward thresholds remain noted as higher and UI-checkable. Strategy/Structure-Busting.md Tactical structure-bust advice and low-GS/effective-structure ideas. Strategy can shift with meta and balance changes. Kept outside Gameplay/reference pages; hard rules stay on Structures/Skulls. Gameplay/Abilities/Grapple.md, Hacking.md, Drain.md Exact formulas and resist thresholds. Duration/amount formulas and resist breakpoints need live verification. Fixed for now: public pages keep stable gates, cooldowns, and technology effects while marking exact formulas as Needs data. Items/Weapons.md Weapon-slot and Elite Rank examples. Some content may be inferred from Elite Rank data rather than directly sourced to Weapons. Fixed for now: page links to Elite Ranks, documents attack readiness broadly, and avoids duplicating slot tables. Items/Specials.md Exact Special formulas, cooldowns, and rank/rarity breakpoints. Discord guide notes identify many Specials, but several need current in-game verification. Added reference list with Needs data flags and links to stable ability pages. Items/Tech-Computers.md Exact Tech Computer stat packages and rarity point values above Rare. Training data gives unlock cadence but not all stat tables. Added deconstruction rules and moved planning advice to Strategy. Strategy/Faction-Ship-Notes.md Wyrd shield/capacitor formulas and faction ship comparisons. Gootchat has useful observations, but they are not yet verified as exact mechanics. Kept as strategy/source notes; faction pages link out instead of treating them as canonical formulas.

docs.title: Training data not yet fully used
docs.text:
The Discord training data contains useful material that is not yet represented as dedicated wiki content:

Topic Possible destination Notes Recommended settings for new players Getting-Started/Settings.md and quick-start guides Added; exact UI labels should still be checked in game after major UI patches. Freegift command and Mark upgrade kit Getting-Started/Chat-Commands.md Added with a command-verification warning because it may change. Lead assist / driver following Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Player (PVP)/Fleet-PvP.md, Strategy/Fleet-PvP.md, and quick-start guides Rules and UI concepts are in Gameplay; tactics are in Strategy. Common click-path workflows Getting-Started/Common-Workflows.md Added for settings, gifts, repairs, travel, Arena, and fleet assist. CPU / computer deconstruction Items/Tech-Computers.md and Strategy/Computer-Deconstruction.md Added reference rules and separate planning guidance. Exact stat tables still need verification. Specials overview Items/Specials.md Added reference list for Technician, Prospector, Advanced Munitions, Scout, Stalker, Advanced Propulsion, Alien Hunter, Hacker, Grappling Hook, Nova, Deflector, Vampire, Battle Ram, Advanced Construct, Drone Master, Advanced Shields, Tank, etc. PvP gearing advice Getting-Started/Quick-Start-Guides/ and Strategy/Fleet-PvP.md Added as guide/community operational material, not universal build law. Structure warfare strategy Strategy/Structure-Busting.md Added as strategy guide; reference rules stay on Structures and Skulls. Mission reward details and refresh behavior Gameplay/Missions.md Discord notes include rewards, refresh limits, and strategy. Verify before adding. Mission planning strategy Strategy/Missions.md Moved out of Gameplay mission reference. Open-world farming and NPC busts Gameplay/Activities/Player vs Environment (PVE)/ plus Strategy/ Rules/reference in Gameplay; tactics and economy guidance in Strategy. Faction/build notes Strategy/Faction-Ship-Notes.md and Strategy/Build-Philosophy.md Added as community guidance, with Wyrd shield mechanics marked for confirmation.

docs.title: Suggested next pass
docs.text:
1. Audit high-risk precise mechanics before expanding them.
  2. Convert unused Discord training data into guide pages, with "community note" labels where needed.
  3. Fill intentionally empty hidden placeholder files only when verified data exists and the page has a clear canonical purpose.

docs.title: Wiki Status
docs.text:
This status page has been retired after the rough-pass buildout. It used to track which first-pass pages existed, but that information is now stale.

Use Wiki Rough-Pass Audit for the current handoff:

  • remaining mechanics that need verification,
  • placeholder pages that are intentionally empty or hidden,
  • source-risk notes,
  • next cleanup or data-gathering steps.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
This guide walks you through the three most common contribution tasks step by step:

  1. Modifying text on an existing page
  2. Adding a new page
  3. Adding a new folder (section)

You do not need to understand the entire system to contribute safely.

docs.title: Modifying text on an existing page
docs.text: This is the easiest and safest way to contribute.

docs.title: Step 1: Find the page
docs.text:
• Use search in Obsidian, or
  • Navigate through folders in the file tree

Open the .md file you want to edit.

docs.title: Step 2: Edit the content
docs.text:
• Change text directly
  • Add or remove sections
  • Fix typos, clarify wording, or expand explanations

Use normal Markdown:

## Section heading

Some text here.

You may also use Markdown Links when linking to other pages:

[[Contributing/FAQ|Frequently Asked Questions]]
[[Contributing/index|Contributing]]

docs.title: Step 3: Save and preview
docs.text:
• Save the file
  • Preview in Obsidian if you want
  • Commit your change when ready

That's it. No navigation changes required.

docs.title: Adding a new page
docs.text: Add a new page when you're introducing new content within an existing section.

docs.title: Step 1: Choose the correct folder
docs.text:
Pick the folder that best matches the topic.

Example:

Gameplay/

If you're unsure, check for similar pages nearby and follow that pattern.

docs.title: Step 2: Create the file
docs.text:
Create a new Markdown file with a clear, descriptive name:

Gameplay/Status Effects.md

Avoid vague names like: - Notes.md - Stuff.md - New Page.md

docs.title: Step 3: Add basic structure
docs.text:
Start with a title and a short introduction:

# Status Effects

This page describes status effects that can be applied to entities in the game.

Add sections as needed.

docs.title: Step 4: Decide if it belongs in navigation
docs.text:
• Important page?
    • Add it explicitly to the folder's .pages file
  • Minor/supporting page?
    • Let it be included automatically via ...

If you don't know, default to automatic.

docs.title: Adding a new folder (section)
docs.text: Add a new folder when you're introducing a new conceptual area, not just a single page.

docs.title: Step 1: Create the folder
docs.text:
Example:

World/Factions/

docs.title: Step 2: Create an index page (required)
docs.text:
Every navigable folder must have an index.md.

World/Factions/index.md

Minimum recommended content:

# Factions

This section documents the major factions that exist in the game world.

The index.md acts as the landing page for the section.

docs.title: Step 3: (Recommended) Add a .pages file
docs.text:
Create:

World/Factions/.pages

Basic template:

nav:
  - index.md
  - ...

This ensures: - The section is clickable - The index page appears first - Future pages are included automatically

docs.title: Step 4: Add pages inside the folder
docs.text:
Example:

World/Factions/
|- index.md
|- The Syndicate.md
|- Free Traders.md
`- .pages

Use wikilinks between related pages where appropriate.

docs.title: How to know if you're doing it right
docs.text:
You're probably on the right track if:

  • You copied an existing pattern
  • Your folder has an index.md
  • You didn't fight the navigation system
  • Your change is easy to understand in isolation

Consistency matters more than perfection.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
For concrete examples you can copy, see:

[[Getting Started/Examples/index|Examples]]

If something here feels unclear or awkward, let us know!

docs.title: Markdown Links
docs.text: This guide explains how to link pages correctly in this wiki, with clear rules for when to use relative vs absolute links. Following these conventions keeps links predictable, portable, and compatible with both MkDocs and Obsidian.

docs.title: The Golden Rule
docs.text:
Links should point to source files, not rendered URLs.

Always link to the .md file path inside docs/, never to a slug like /hello-world.

docs.title: Link Types at a Glance
docs.text: Situation Use Example Same folder Relative page.md Child folder Relative Folder/index.md Parent folder Relative ../index.md Distant section Absolute /Section/Page.md Templates / global refs Absolute /Contributing/index.md

docs.title: When to use them
docs.text:
Use relative links when linking to pages that are:

  • In the same folder
  • In a child or parent folder
  • Part of the same section that might be moved together

docs.title: Why
docs.text:
• Mirror the file system
  • Survive navigation changes
  • Work in both MkDocs and Obsidian
  • Easy to reason about

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Assume this file:

docs/World/hello.md

Same folder

[Maps](maps.md)

Child folder

[Planets](Planets/index.md)

Parent folder

[World overview](../index.md)

Sibling folder

[Ships](../Ships/index.md)

[Tip] Tip: If you could reach it with cd in a terminal, you can link to it relatively.

docs.title: When to use them
docs.text:
Use absolute links when:

  • Linking across distant sections
  • Referring to canonical pages (FAQ, Contributing, Index pages)
  • Writing templates or examples meant to be copied

docs.title: Format
docs.text:
Absolute links always start at the docs/ root:

/Section/Page.md

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
From a page in Contributing/Getting Started/, use relative paths to other sections:

[Contributing guide](../index.md)
[FAQ](../FAQ.md)
[Ship classes](../../Ships/index.md)

From the docs/ root, links are simply Section/Page.md (e.g. Ships/index.md, Entities/Currencies/index.md).

docs.title: Filenames or folders with spaces
docs.text: Use the literal space in the path so the link matches the file or folder name on disk (e.g. Tech Points.md, Jump Gates.md, Player vs Player (PVP)/Arena.md). Do not use %20 in internal links -- that can break resolution in some builds.

docs.title: Don't link to rendered URLs
docs.text:
/hello-world

Rendered slugs depend on titles and theme behavior and may change.

docs.title: Recommended Style Rules (Summary)
docs.text:
• Prefer relative links within a section
  • Use absolute links for site-wide references
  • Always link to the .md file, not a rendered URL
  • Do not rely on titles or slugs for navigation

docs.title: One-Sentence Mantra
docs.text: If you can trace the file path with your eyes, use a relative link. If you want a global reference, use an absolute one.

docs.title: External Links (Off-Wiki References)
docs.text: Markdown links can also point to external websites (outside the wiki). While these are sometimes necessary, they should be used sparingly and intentionally.

docs.title: When External Links Are Appropriate
docs.text:
Use an external link when:

  • The information is authoritative and primary (e.g., official developer posts, patch notes, or APIs).
  • The content is too large or out of scope to reasonably mirror on the wiki.
  • The link provides verification, not opinion (e.g., source citations).

Example:

For official patch notes, see the [developer announcement](https://example.com/patch-notes).

docs.title: Why External Links Should Be Minimized
docs.text:
Excessive external linking can introduce problems:

  • Bias: Linking selectively to certain creators, forums, or communities can imply endorsement or favoritism.
  • Link rot: External pages can change, move, or disappear over time.
  • Fragmented knowledge: Readers are pushed off-site instead of finding answers within the wiki.

As a rule:

If the information can live on the wiki, it should live on the wiki.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Prefer internal pages over external links whenever possible.
  • If an external link is needed, ensure it is:
    • Neutral and factual
    • Clearly labeled (what the reader should expect)
    • Not required to understand the core content
  • Avoid linking to:
    • Opinion pieces
    • Monetized content (ads, referral links)
    • Single-person interpretations when a neutral summary can be written instead

docs.title: Recommended Pattern
docs.text:
Write the content locally, then optionally add a source:

This mechanic was introduced in version 1.4 to rebalance fleet combat. 

*Source: [Official patch notes](https://example.com/patch-notes)*

This keeps the wiki self-contained, neutral, and durable, while still allowing readers to verify information if they choose to.

docs.title: Using Markdown
docs.text: This page provides canonical examples for writing and formatting content on the The Infinite Black 2 Wiki. All contributors should follow these conventions to keep the wiki clean, consistent, and professional.

docs.title: Headings
docs.text:
Use headings to structure pages logically. Do not skip levels.

# Page Title (H1 - only one per page)

## Major Section (H2)

### Subsection (H3)

#### Minor Details (H4)

Rules - Each page must have exactly one H1 at the top. - Use sentence case for headings. - Avoid emojis in headings (allowed in body text if appropriate).

Paragraphs & Line Breaks

Write in clear, concise paragraphs.

This is a paragraph. It should explain a single idea clearly.

This is a new paragraph. Avoid walls of text.

Do not manually add line breaks inside paragraphs.

docs.title: Emphasis
docs.text:
*Italic*  
**Bold**  
***Bold Italic***

Guidelines - Use bold for important terms or first mentions. - Use italic sparingly (titles, emphasis, flavor).

docs.title: Unordered Lists
docs.text:
- Fast 
- Durable
- Expensive

docs.title: Ordered Lists
docs.text:
1. Warp to target 
2. Engage shields 
3. Deploy drones

docs.title: Nested Lists
docs.text:
- Weapons
  - Lasers
  - Missiles
- Defenses  
  - Shields  
  - Armor

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
We use standard markdown links, because the tech lead couldn't figure out how to configure wikilinks plugin with mkdocs properly. If you'd like to attempt it, contact the owners of this wiki.

See Markdown Links for examples of how to link pages.

docs.title: Naming Conventions (Pages)
docs.text: Consistency matters more than perfection.

docs.title: Page Titles
docs.text:
• Use Title Case
  • Be descriptive, not vague

Good - Carrier - Ship Classes - Harvest Drones - Faction: The Pirates

Avoid - car - Ships2 - Stuff - Guide_final_v3

docs.title: Inline Code
docs.text:
Use for commands, keys, or filenames.

Press `F` to interact. Edit the `mkdocs.yml` file.

docs.title: Code Blocks
docs.text:
site_name: The Infinite Black 2 Wiki 
theme:   
  name: material

docs.title: Admonitions (Info Boxes)
docs.text:
Material for MkDocs supports admonitions.

!!! info
    This ship excels at hit-and-run tactics.

!!! warning
    This information may change in future patches.

!!! danger
    Using multiple accounts may result in a ban.

docs.title: Collapsible Sections (Spoilers)
docs.text:
Use for spoilers or optional deep dives.

??? spoiler "Story Spoilers"
    The player captain is a mass-produced clone with no moral compass.

docs.title: Tone & Style
docs.text:
• Write in neutral, informative language
  • Avoid speculation unless clearly labeled
  • No first-person opinions (I think, In my experience)
  • Assume the reader is intelligent but new

Good

This weapon is commonly used in PvP engagements due to its high burst damage.

Avoid

I think this weapon is really good and I always use it.

docs.title: Final Notes
docs.text:
• When in doubt, copy an existing good page
  • Consistency beats cleverness
  • Ask before inventing new conventions

If something isn't covered here, bring it up with the project lead.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: List examples here!

docs.title: Example Map Page
docs.text: This example is intentionally minimal. Use it as a structure reference, not as game data.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: One or two sentences explaining what the map is.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text: Link to canonical pages for shared mechanics such as Sectors and Security Rating.

docs.title: Needs Data
docs.text: List missing values here instead of guessing.

docs.title: Example Ship Page
docs.text: This example is intentionally minimal. Use it as a structure reference, not as game data.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: One or two sentences explaining what the ship is for.

docs.title: Mechanics
docs.text: Link to canonical pages for shared mechanics such as Size and Targeting, Equip Points, and Ship Mods.

docs.title: Needs Data
docs.text: List missing values here instead of guessing.

docs.title: Entities
docs.text: Entities are the organizations, currencies, and structures that shape the world of The Infinite Black 2: corporations and their alliances, the currencies and resources you earn and spend, and the structures (planets, garrisons, outposts, shipyards) that corps deploy for territory and rewards.

docs.title: Sections
docs.text: Section Summary Corporations Join a corp for tech, ships, and protection. Alliances, Corporate Defenders, and how corps work. Currencies Credits, Skulls, Tech Points, Star Shards, and Relics -- what they are and how to get them. Structures Defense units: Planets, Garrisons, Outposts, Shipyards. Deployment, PvP, and Artifacts.

docs.title: How they connect
docs.text:
• Corporations hold structures and spend Tech Points to unlock more. They use Skulls and Relics to build ships at Shipyards.
  • Structures store Artifacts (corp tech bonus; study for +1 Skill Point) and capture Relic Shrines via Garrisons.
  • Currencies fund everything: corps, upgrades, Quick Jump, and the in-game economy.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Ships -- What you build at corp Shipyards and equip with items
  • World / Maps -- Where structures deploy and territory is contested
  • PvP -- Skulls and structure warfare

docs.title: Corporations
docs.text:
Corporations are the social backbone of The Infinite Black 2. Most game features involve working as a team: unlocking technology, capturing artifacts, harvesting relics, and fighting in the Alliance War.

New players

Corporations provide technology, skills, ship construction access, shared missions, and group protection. New-player routing belongs in How To Play.

docs.title: Leveling and skills
docs.text: Corporations earn XP from every kill, then level up and train skills just like players. A corporation's skill and technology choices apply to all ships owned by its members.

docs.title: Starting structures
docs.text:
Every corporation begins with:

  • One Planet
  • One Garrison
  • One Outpost
  • One Shipyard

These defense units are leveled up using resource deposits, which unlock more Equip Points and gear slots. Defense units may be deployed in PvP system maps to grow corporation and alliance territory.

docs.title: Unlocking more structures
docs.text: Corporation members can invest Tech Points to unlock additional defense units from the Corporation Management panel. See the Structures overview for limits and Structure Reference for cap-cost tables.

docs.title: Corporate Defenders
docs.text: Ships owned by corp members can be summoned as Corporate Defenders to protect defense units under attack. They deploy automatically and fight under the game's AI -- and recall if the owner logs in.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text: Topic Description Alliances Multiple corps in one team; shared chat, missions, and defenders. Corporate Defenders Your ships defending corp structures while you're offline. Structures Planets, Garrisons, Outposts, Shipyards -- limits and deployment. Shipyards & ship building Building ships for corp members; faction choice and blueprints.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• How To Play -- Why joining a corp is one of your first goals
  • Ships -- What you build at corp Shipyards
  • Currencies -- Tech Points, Skulls, Credits
  • Maps -- Where corps deploy and fight for territory

docs.title: Alliances
docs.text: Several corporations can join together as a single team, forming an alliance that captures territory and Relics in a never-ending galactic war.

docs.title: What Alliances Share
docs.text:
• Chat room -- One alliance-wide chat.
  • Missions -- Shared alliance missions (progress and rewards).
  • Corporate Defenders -- The alliance shares one pool of defender ships; any eligible ship in the alliance can be called to any defense unit under attack.

docs.title: Alliance War
docs.text:
Alliances fight over PvP maps, defense units, Relic Shrines, and Artifacts. Most stable territory mechanics are documented on their dedicated pages:

Mechanic Canonical page Structure deployment, shields, Quick Jump, recall Structures and Deployment Relic Shrine capture and Alliance Points Relics Structure attack/defense rules Structure Warfare Rules Structure attack/defense strategy Structure Busting Strategy Fleet movement, drivers, sort calls Fleet PvP Rules Fleet tactics Fleet PvP Strategy

docs.title: PvP and Friends
docs.text: All players in an alliance are friendly in PvP systems -- you don't attack each other in open-world PvP. This does not apply in The Arena, where matchmaking is separate.

docs.title: Alliance Citizens
docs.text:
Alliance Citizens are players who:

  • Are Level 25+ in a corporation in the alliance, and
  • Have logged in within the last 15 days.

docs.title: Limits
docs.text:
Primary server Universe #2 Max corporations per alliance 6 6 Max Alliance Citizens (total) 180 100

Corporations cannot invite new players once the alliance citizen count is maxed. They can always invite players under Level 25 (they don't count until they hit 25). Players who haven't logged in for 15 days do not count toward the cap.

docs.title: Primary Alliance Citizen
docs.text: Each player has a single Primary Alliance Citizen character -- their highest-level character. This is used for mechanics that depend on "one main per alliance," e.g. capturing Security Rating 8 maps (citizen requirements).

docs.title: Management
docs.text:
• Click the STATS button on the Corp Panel to view alliance and citizen details.
  • Corporations can transfer Corp Tech Points to other corporations in the alliance. Use :ALLIANCE in chat for details.
  • Alliance Leaders (Commanders of the primary corporation) see Invite and Kick buttons on other corps' panels. Member corporations see a Leave Alliance button.

When someone joins or leaves

All players in the alliance are stunned for 30 seconds when a corp joins or leaves. Changes cannot be made while defense units are under attack.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Corporations
  • Corporate Defenders
  • Structures -- Deploying in PvP maps
  • Structure Warfare Rules -- Structure attack and defense rules
  • Structure Busting Strategy -- Community structure warfare guidance
  • Relics -- What alliances fight for at shrines

docs.title: Corporate Defender Ships
docs.text: Ships owned by members of a corporation can be summoned while the player is offline to help protect defense units under attack. These Corporate Defenders are deployed automatically and fight under the game's AI. If the ship's owner logs in, their defender ships recall.

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• No penalty for dying -- Corp Defenders killed during an attack do not lose durability or suffer other penalties.
  • Shared pool -- Alliances share one pool of Corp Defenders. Any eligible ship in the alliance can be called to fight at any defense unit in the alliance.
  • Auto deployment -- The strongest available defenders deploy first, with a mix of small and large units.

docs.title: Eligibility
docs.text:
A ship can be used as a Corporate Defender only if it meets all of:

Requirement Value Level 10 or higher Weapon Must have a weapon equipped Gear Score 1,000 or higher

Hardcore ships

Hardcore ships are never used as Corp Defenders.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Corporations
  • Alliances
  • Structures -- What defenders protect
  • Ships

docs.title: Currencies
docs.text: The Infinite Black 2 uses several currencies and key resources. Only Credits and Skulls are tradable between players; the rest are bound to your account.

docs.title: Primary Currencies
docs.text: Currency Tradable? Summary Credits Yes Standard economy currency. Trading, raffles, converting to Tech Points. Skulls Yes PvP currency. Ship crafting, Elite Ranks, ship durability repair. Tech Points No Progression and utility. Item upgrades, corps, bank slots, Quick Jump, store. Star Shards No Premium currency. Cosmetics only (skins, trails, avatars).

docs.title: Crafting & Territory Resources
docs.text:
Resource Tradable? Summary Relics Yes Rare ship-crafting component. From Alliance Wars (shrines), Arena, level rewards, loot.

Market note

Exchange rates between tradable currencies fluctuate with player activity. Check current market listings, trade chat, and your corporation instead of treating old wiki or Discord values as current prices.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Corporations -- Tech Points for structures; Skulls and Relics for ship building
  • Ships -- What you spend Skulls and Relics on
  • Getting Started -- How to get Skulls, Tech Points, and Credits

docs.title: Credits
docs.text: Credits are the primary standard currency in The Infinite Black 2. They are the lifeblood of the player economy and are fully tradable between players.

docs.title: How to Obtain
docs.text:
Credits are generated through commerce and recycling:

  • Auction House -- Sell items, resources, or equipment to other players.
  • Scrapping -- Break down "Junk" items or scrap piles of crafted resources.
  • Harvesting and resource stacks -- Harvest resources, create stacks from deposited resources, then sell or scrap those stacks.
  • PvE busts and farming -- NPC structure/planet busts and open-world farming can produce items worth scrapping or selling.
  • Trading -- Direct trades with other players (including Skulls for Credits).

docs.title: Uses
docs.text:
Use Description Auction House Buy items, ships, and gear listed by other players. Raffles Buy tickets for unique ship raffles. Tech Point conversion Convert Credits into Tech Points to fuel progression.

Conversion rate

1,000 Credits -> 1 Tech Point (at any time)

docs.title: Market caveats
docs.text:
TIB2 does not provide a durable market-history table in this wiki. Player prices move over time, and old Discord prices should be treated as dated observations rather than current reference values.

For live prices:

  • Ask active players or your corporation.
  • Check current market listings.
  • Treat unusual market-chat values as context, not a rule.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Tech Points -- What Credits convert into
  • Skulls -- The other tradable currency; often exchanged for Credits
  • Items -- What you buy and sell on the Auction House
  • Open World Farming

docs.title: Relics
docs.text:
Relics are a rare ship crafting component used to build Assassin, Hades, Carrier, and Emperor-class ships. Each alien faction has its own relic type for building that faction's ships at Corporation Shipyards.

Relics are tradable like other resources -- they can be sold, traded, or used yourself.

docs.title: How to Obtain
docs.text: Source Notes Alliance Wars Corps capture Relic Shrines with Garrisons; shrines generate relics. Level rewards Every 5 levels starting at Level 15. The Arena Arena rewards can include relics. Loot Dropped by NPCs. Free Daily Gifts / Holiday Events Special events and daily rewards.

docs.title: Relic Shrines (Alliance Wars)
docs.text:
• Each corporation may control 4 Relic Shrines; max 10 shrines total per alliance.
  • Captured shrines generate one relic at 100% progress. Time per 1% progress:
Shrine size Minutes per 1% Massive 78 Huge 86 Large 95 Other 104

docs.title: Alliance Points (relic distribution)
docs.text:
Each player has an Alliance Point counter that increases with kills, PvP, defense fights, Arena, and other alliance glory. Players with the most Alliance Points get priority when alliance relic loot is distributed. When someone receives a relic, their counter resets so others get a chance.

Captain Panel -> STATS to see your current Alliance Points.

docs.title: Using Relics
docs.text:
• USE a relic item to add it to your crafting bank.
  • BUILD a relic via Captain Panel -> Item Crafting (same crafting panel as items).

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Ship Crafting -- What relics are used for
  • Skulls -- The other key ship-crafting currency
  • Corporations -- Shipyards and shrine capture
  • Alliances -- Shared relic generation and Alliance Points

docs.title: Skulls
docs.text: Skulls are a currency and crafting resource awarded to Captains only from Player-vs-Player (PvP) activity. They are tradable and used throughout the game for high-end progression.

docs.title: The Arena
docs.text:
The Arena is a controlled PvP environment with balanced teams and no Artifacts or Corporation Skills. Rounds run every few hours and award Skulls.

  • Minimum: One ship with Gear Score of 1,000 and no free starting gear. Some arenas require more than one ship.
  • Winning: The team with the most kills wins. High kill counts can give bonus rewards even if your team loses.
  • No durability loss on death in The Arena; gear loses only 1% durability, and only half the time.

Access via Captain Panel -> ARENA when registration is open. Arena starts every few hours at the top of the hour.

docs.title: Open-world PvP and structure warfare
docs.text:
• Open-world PvP -- Destroying enemy player ships in non-safe sectors.
  • Structure warfare -- Bonus Skulls are awarded for defending or attacking non-NPC defense units. Join a Corporation and get involved in the Alliance Wars to earn more.
  • Structure kills -- Structure kill payout is based on structure Gear Score: Outposts/Shipyards pay 1 Skull per 500 GS, Garrisons pay 1 per 400 GS, and Planets pay 1 per 300 GS.

docs.title: Player kill credit
docs.text:
Open-world player-kill Skull credit works this way:

Condition Skull credit You get the killing shot You gain 1 Skull. You attacked the target while it was in combat You also gain 1 Skull when it dies. Your corporation gets the kill Your corporation gains 1 Skull.

Additional observed bonuses can apply:

Bonus condition Extra Skulls Weekend event +1 Daily double-Skulls PvP event +1 Defending an engaged structure +2 Attacking an engaged structure +1 Fighting at an engaged structure against a high-Gear-Score killed ship +1

Needs confirmation

Open-world bonus values can change. Verify in game after PvP reward changes.

docs.title: Uses
docs.text: Use Description Ship crafting Required (with Relics) to build advanced ship blueprints at corp Shipyards. Elite Ranks Spent on the Unit Inspect Panel to upgrade ships and defense structures. Ship durability repair Use the REPAIR button on the ship inspection panel to restore durability (consumes Skulls).

docs.title: Ship Elite Rank costs (Skulls)
docs.text:
Rank Cost 1 100 2 500 3 1,000 4 2,000 5 3,000

Defense unit Elite Rank costs are 5x the ship costs.

docs.title: Market Value
docs.text: Skulls are needed for crafting and Elite Ranks. Exact prices are market-dependent and should be checked in game rather than treated as fixed wiki data.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• The Arena
  • Structure Warfare Rules
  • Corporations -- Structure warfare and Alliance Wars
  • Elite Ranks
  • Relics -- The other key ship-crafting resource
  • Credits -- Converting Skulls to Credits (or vice versa)

docs.title: Star Shards
docs.text:
Star Shards are the premium cosmetic currency. They are used only for visual customization and do not affect gameplay stats.

Tradability: Star Shards are bound to your account.

docs.title: How to Obtain
docs.text: Source Details TIB Market (exchange) Convert Tech Points into Star Shards. 1,000 Tech Points -> 2,000 Star Shards (once per day). Gameplay Earned from Outpost mission chains and defeating the final boss at the end of the chain. Store Purchasable directly for real money in the cash shop.

docs.title: Uses
docs.text: • Cosmetics -- Purchase visual upgrades from the TIB Market: ship skins, engine trails, avatars, and similar items.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Tech Points -- Convert into Star Shards
  • Currencies overview

docs.title: Tech Points
docs.text:
Tech Points are the main utility and progression currency. They are used for corps, item upgrades, bank space, travel, and more. You earn thousands over your career and can convert 1,000 Credits into 1 Tech Point at any time.

Tradability: Tech Points are bound -- they cannot be traded between players.

docs.title: How to Obtain
docs.text:
• Conversion -- 1,000 Credits = 1 Tech Point (any time).
  • Gameplay -- Rewards from Missions, outpost missions, and achievements.
  • Store -- Purchasable at Spellbook.com to support the game. Purchases also grant Reward Points -- open Captain Panel -> REWARDS for bonus gifts (Party Boxes, skins, etc.).

Oblivion Server

Tech Point orders deliver to both the Primary Server and Oblivion Server if you have a character on each. There is no link between servers and nothing transfers between them.

docs.title: Uses: Equipment & Items
docs.text:
• Item upgrades -- Rank items up or down; upgrade rarity (e.g. Rare -> Ultra-Rare) via the item inspect panel.
  • Research -- Buy research time for technologies in the captain research menu.

docs.title: Uses: Player Convenience
docs.text:
• Bank & garage -- Unlock additional Item Bank and Ship Garage slots.
  • Quick Jump -- Spend Tech Points to Quick Jump to discovered locations (e.g. alliance structures, human jump gates). Cost is 1 Tech Point per jump regardless of how many ships move.

docs.title: Uses: Corporations
docs.text:
• Structures -- Unlock new defense units and pay to deploy them (Corporation Management panel).
  • Member slots -- Buy additional corp member slots.
  • Corp bank -- Purchase extra corp bank slots for shared storage.
  • Creating a corporation -- Tech Points are required to found a corp.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Credits -- Converting to Tech Points
  • Corporations -- Corp uses of Tech Points
  • Structures -- Deployment and unlocks
  • Item Upgrades -- Rarity, rank, quality, and repair uses
  • Tech Points Quick Start -- cattabliss' practical TP spending guide

docs.title: Structures Overview
docs.text:
Structures, also called defense units, are corporation-owned entities used for territory, ship construction, artifacts, relics, and structure warfare.

Captains earn Skulls for both defending and attacking non-NPC structures.

Gear Score requirement

Defense units must have 3,000+ Gear Score or be recalled! Equip them properly or they will be forced back.

docs.title: Corporation Limits
docs.text:
Every corporation starts with one of each type and can unlock more with Tech Points (Corporation Management panel).

Structure Start Max Notes Planets 1 1 Central hub; system-wide tech bonus; respawn option. Garrisons 1 4 Garrison Strike; only structure that can capture Relic Shrines. Outposts 1 8 Territory growth; movement/resist bonus for corp in range. Shipyards 1 4 Build ships for corp members; one faction per shipyard.

docs.title: How Defense Units Fight
docs.text:
• Firepower -- Defense units are much stronger than a single ship. Many defense-unit stats use large multipliers.
  • Weapons & drones -- Attack faster than their listed speed. Slower weapons receive a larger attack-speed bonus.
  • Targeting -- Defenses ignore ship size targeting limits and target nearly instantly.
  • Stat multipliers -- All defense units: +Splash Chance x10, Battery Capacity x10, Battery Recharge x4. No 10% auxiliary item penalty like ships. Weapons and fighter drones attack x6 faster than normal.
  • Leveling -- Level up defense units with resources from their inspect menu.

docs.title: Defense Shields (PvP)
docs.text:
When any defense unit is attacked:

  • 90-second immunity -- The structure (and all ships in the sector) get a 90-second shield so defenders can organize. All ships in sector are immune to PvP during this period, including attackers.
  • 1-hour shield -- After enough attackers die, a 1-hour protection shield is raised to give defenders a break.
  • :ALLIANCE SHIELD -- Alliance leaders can type this in chat to raise a 4-hour shield once per day.

docs.title: Quick Jump
docs.text: General Quick Jump rules are documented on Maps. Structure warfare has additional attack-state rules; see Structure Warfare Rules.

docs.title: Loot & Drops
docs.text:
• NPC defenses -- Drop high-quality items and rare crafting components.
  • All defenses -- Drop hundreds or thousands of resources when destroyed.
  • Non-NPC defenses -- Players earn Skulls for killing them.

docs.title: PvP structure Skull payout
docs.text:
When a non-NPC structure is destroyed in PvP, players who attacked it earn Skulls based on the structure's Gear Score:

Structure Skull payout Outpost / Shipyard 1 Skull per 500 Gear Score Garrison 1 Skull per 400 Gear Score Planet 1 Skull per 300 Gear Score

For structure attack/defense strategy, see Structure Busting Strategy.

docs.title: PvE Deployment
docs.text: Defenses can be deployed in human-controlled PvE maps but recall automatically after 15 days. See Deployment for full rules.

docs.title: Structure Types
docs.text:
Structure Summary Planets One per corp. Respawn option; system-wide tech; x40 Hull/Shield; Sacred Relic Shrine = alliance-wide tech. Garrisons Up to 4. Garrison Strike (area attack); x30 Hull/Shield; capture Relic Shrines. Outposts Up to 8. Territory growth every 6h; x12 Hull/Shield; bonuses for corp in territory. Shipyards Up to 4. Build ships; unlock blueprints; x12 Hull/Shield.

Corporations store Artifacts at planets and garrisons for tech bonuses; any player can study an artifact for +1 Skill Point.

For structure caps, slot tables, Equip Point formulas, and default technologies, see Structure Reference.

docs.title: NPC Factions & Behavior
docs.text: Faction Behavior Notes Human Non-hostile; accept deposits Garrisons at Jump Gates can be Quick Jump targets once discovered. Human Outposts/Shipyards roam. Wyrd, Hets, Precursors, Pirates Hostile Structures spawn in PvP maps and Security 9 PvE maps controlled by that faction.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Deployment -- Costs, timers, blocking, PvE recall
  • Structure Warfare Rules -- PvP structure rules
  • Structure Busting Strategy -- Community structure warfare guidance
  • Corporations -- Unlocking and managing structures
  • Relics -- Shrines and Garrison capture
  • Skulls -- Earning from structure warfare
  • Artifacts -- Stored at planets and garrisons; corp tech and study bonus

docs.title: Artifacts
docs.text: Artifacts are mysterious alien objects scattered across the universe in The Infinite Black 2. There are over 50 of them. Corporations capture and store them at planets and garrisons to gain technology benefits. Any player can study an artifact to permanently gain +1 Skill Point.

docs.title: Corporation Control and Tech Bonus
docs.text:
When a corporation controls an artifact (by having it stored at one of their planets or garrisons):

  • All members of that corporation receive the artifact's technology bonus.
  • This applies regardless of the member's Skill Tree investment or unlocked tech research -- the bonus is global for the corp while they hold the artifact.
  • If the corporation loses control of the artifact (e.g. it is captured or the structure is destroyed), the tech bonus is lost for that corp. The bonus is only active while they control it.

So capturing and holding artifacts is a major goal for corps that want to boost their entire membership.

docs.title: Studying Artifacts (Skill Points)
docs.text:
Any player can STUDY an artifact. Studying is a one-time, permanent action:

  • Studying an artifact permanently awards +1 Skill Point to that captain.
  • This is a long-term goal: many players seek out artifacts over the course of the game to collect every possible bonus skill point.

docs.title: Who Can Study?
docs.text:
• Uncaptured artifacts -- Any player can study them.
  • Corporation-captured artifacts -- Only alliance members of the corporation that holds the artifact can study it. This is both a perk for joining that corp/alliance and an incentive for outsiders to attack and liberate artifacts so they can study them too.

Once a player has studied an artifact, the +1 Skill Point is kept forever -- even if the corp later loses the artifact. Only the corp tech bonus is tied to control; the personal skill bonus is permanent.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Aspect Detail Count 50+ artifacts in the universe Stored at Corp Planets and Garrisons Corp benefit Tech bonus for all corp members while controlled Player benefit Study = +1 Skill Point (permanent) Study access Anyone if uncaptured; only alliance members if corp-captured

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Structures -- Planets, Garrisons, and where artifacts are stored
  • Corporations -- How corps capture and benefit from artifacts
  • Technology -- What the artifact tech bonus affects
  • Skills -- What Skill Points do

docs.title: Structure Deployment
docs.text: Deploying Corporation structures is a strategic action that allows you to claim territory and set up forward operating bases.

docs.title: Deployment Costs & Mechanics
docs.text:
• Cost: 15 Tech Points per deployment.
  • Duration: Structures are permanent until recalled or destroyed (see PvE Exceptions below).
  • Movement: Structures are static once deployed. To move them, they must be recalled and re-deployed.

docs.title: Recall Process
docs.text:
Recalling a structure returns it to the Corporation inventory. Recalls are immediately stopped if a structure is combat flagged in an engagement.

  • Timer: Recalling takes exactly 3 minutes.
    • Note: This timer may fluctuate slightly (by a few seconds) depending on server health/tick rate.

docs.title: Restrictions & Blocking
docs.text: You cannot deploy a structure just anywhere. The following conditions must be met:

docs.title: 1. Sector Exclusivity
docs.text: Only one structure of each type can exist per sector. (e.g., You cannot place a Garrison in a sector that already has a Garrison).

docs.title: 2. Proximity Blocking
docs.text:
Deployment is blocked if Enemy Entities are present in the current sector or any connected sector (1-sector radius).

  • Blocking Entities: Enemy Structures (Player or NPC) and Enemy Player Ships.
  • Non-Blocking Entities: NPC Ships.

docs.title: 3. Combat Flag
docs.text: You cannot deploy structures while your active ship has a Combat Flag. * Workaround: You may select a different, unflagged ship to initiate the deployment.

docs.title: PvE Map Specifics
docs.text:
In PvE maps, to prevent clutter and stagnation, specific limits apply:

  • Map Capacity: There is a limit of 7 Player-Owned Structures per map.
  • Auto-Recall: Structures in PvE maps will automatically recall after 15 Days.

docs.title: Garrisons
docs.text: Garrisons are powerful defensive structures. Each corporation can have up to 4 Garrisons. They provide a Garrison Strike area attack and are the only structure that can capture Relic Shrines.

docs.title: Combat & Stats
docs.text: Feature Details Garrison Strike Area attack similar to Nova explosions. Damage is based on the Garrison's level and Max Equip Points. Hull/Shield +Max Hull and +Max Shield stats are multiplied by x30. Relic Shrines Relic Shrines can only be captured by placing a Garrison on them. Corps deploy Garrisons on shrines to harvest Relics for their alliance.

docs.title: Human Garrisons (PvE)
docs.text:
In PvE maps, the Human faction has Garrisons to aid travel:

  • Location: Always at Jump Gates.
  • Quick Jump: You can Quick Jump to these Garrisons if you've discovered them.

docs.title: Elite Ranks
docs.text: Higher-tier Garrisons need Skulls for Elite versions and offer much better durability and firepower. Defense unit Elite Rank costs are 5x ship costs (see Skulls).

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
• Owner: Corporation (player or NPC).
  • Cost: 15 Tech Points.
  • Rules: See Deployment.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Structures overview
  • Relics -- Shrines and capture
  • PvP -- Structure warfare and Skulls

docs.title: Jump Gates
docs.text: Jump Gates are static, neutral structures that connect solar systems. You use them to move between maps -- you cannot cross a map edge without a Jump Gate.

docs.title: Ownership and Behavior
docs.text:
• Ownership: Jump Gates are not owned by any player or NPC.
  • Permanent: They exist as fixed points linking specific systems.
  • Combat: Jump Gates cannot be attacked or destroyed.

docs.title: Safety (PvP)
docs.text:
In PvP maps, you are relatively safe at Jump Gates between systems -- but only if you are not flagged in combat. If you are in combat, you can still be attacked at the gate.

Never 100% safe in PvP

In PvP space you are never fully safe; gates only provide relative safety when not in combat.

docs.title: Quick Jump
docs.text: From the hamburger menu you can Quick Jump to eligible destinations. Human Garrisons located at discovered Jump Gates can be Quick Jump targets. See Maps for the canonical rule list.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• World & Maps -- Starmap and navigation
  • PvP -- Safety at gates
  • Garrisons -- Human garrisons at Jump Gates

docs.title: Outposts
docs.text: Outposts expand territory and support corp members in the area. Each corporation can have up to 8 Outposts.

docs.title: Territory & Bonuses
docs.text:
Feature Details Territory growth Outposts grow a sphere of influence by 1 sector every 6 hours, up to a maximum of (2 + Outpost_Level / 5) sectors. Corp members in territory Movement and resist bonuses. Enemies in territory Slower and less protected. Hull/Shield +Max Hull and +Max Shield stats are multiplied by x12.

Quick Jump

Attack-response Quick Jump appears to exclude Outposts, but player-owned alliance Outpost edge cases need confirmation. Keep Outpost jump rules linked to Maps and Structure Warfare Rules until verified in game.

docs.title: Human Outposts (PvE)
docs.text:
• Roaming: Human Outposts randomly roam maps.
  • Travel: Human Outposts are not discovered Jump Gate targets.
  • Services: Non-hostile; they accept resource deposits.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
• Cost: 15 Tech Points.
  • Rules: See Deployment.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Structures overview
  • Corporations
  • Deployment

docs.title: Planets
docs.text: Planets are the central hub of every corporation. Each corporation may own one Planet.

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text: Benefit Details Respawn Members can choose to respawn at the Corporate Planet instead of Earth: Settings -> Gameplay Menu. Technology Planets grant a system-wide technology bonus to all corp members. Stats +Max Hull and +Max Shield stats are multiplied by x40 on the planet. Sacred Relic Shrine If a planet is deployed on a Sacred Relic Shrine, its technology bonus applies to the entire alliance throughout the entire game.

docs.title: Corporation Planets
docs.text:
• Limit: One planet per corporation.
  • Role: Central economic and social hub; primary respawn option for members.

docs.title: Unique Faction Homeworlds (NPC)
docs.text:
Specific NPC factions have permanent Homeworlds at coordinates (7, 7) in their Security 9 maps:

Planet Faction Map Earth Human Sol -- starting point for all new players. Tortuga Pirates Dorado Eye'lr'tas'ka Hets Vega The Life Seed Wyrd Rigel Arc Prime Precursors Orion

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
• Cost: 15 Tech Points.
  • Rules: See Deployment.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Structures overview
  • Corporations
  • Relics -- Shrines and Sacred Relic Shrine

docs.title: Shipyards
docs.text: Shipyards construct new ships for corporation members and unlock new blueprints as they level up. Each corporation can have up to 4 Shipyards.

docs.title: Capacity & Factions
docs.text: Feature Details Limit Up to 4 Shipyards per corporation. Faction Each Shipyard is dedicated to one faction (Pirate, Het, Wyrd, Precursor). Corp leadership chooses the faction; that Shipyard then builds that faction's ships. Hull/Shield +Max Hull and +Max Shield stats are multiplied by x12.

docs.title: Function
docs.text:
• Building ships -- Members build ships at the corp's Shipyards when the correct level and blueprint are unlocked. Advanced ships require Skulls and Relics in addition to resources.
  • Blueprints -- Unlocked as the Shipyard levels up (via resources in the inspect menu).
  • Storage -- Shipyards also store fleet vessels.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
• Cost: 15 Tech Points.
  • Rules: See Deployment.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Structures overview
  • Ship Crafting
  • Corporations
  • Skulls and Relics

Defense-unit Equip Points scale from structure type, Hardcore status, level, and Elite Rank.

Structure Normal base Hardcore base Level bonus Elite Rank bonus Planet 30 40 floor(level / 10) +5 per rank Garrison 20 30 floor(level / 10) +4 per rank Outpost 15 25 floor(level / 10) +3 per rank Shipyard 15 25 floor(level / 10) +3 per rank

Total Equip Points are the base value plus the level bonus and Elite Rank bonus.
